blocks.md: Square brackets select elements. `v[i]` picks item `i` (counting from 1 by default). The index can itself be a vector, picking several items at once.
A matrix is indexed with a semicolon separating the axes: `M[row;col]`. Leave one side blank to take a whole row or column.
You can also **assign through** an index to change selected elements in place. The `⋄` (diamond) separates two statements on one line so we can change then show.

blocks.prompt: Select the third element of 10 20 30 40 50. Expect 30.
From the first ten integers, pick the elements at positions 1 3 5 7 9, giving 1 3 5 7 9.
blocks.hint: the index can be a vector

blocks.prompt: From the 3×3 grid of 1 to 9, select the element in row 2, column 3. Expect 6.
From the 3×3 grid of 1 to 9, take the whole second row, giving 4 5 6.
blocks.hint: leave the column side of the semicolon empty

blocks.prompt: Start from a vector of five zeros, set positions 2 and 4 to 9, and show the result 0 9 0 9 0. Use ⋄ to separate statements.
blocks.hint: assign into v[2 4] then evaluate v
